new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by a spirit that is arrack: manufactured using a special yeast from sugar-cane molasses or from molasses of sweet plant juices combined with rice, distinguishing it from raki and from rum/sugar-cane spirits of heading 2208 40.
Spirit production process in which fermented sugar-cane molasses or sweet plant-juice molasses together with rice, using a special yeast, are distilled to produce arrack.

new_classes.label_de: Arrak, in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von höchstens 2 l (KN 2208 90 11)
new_classes.definition_en: Arrack — a spirit manufactured using a special yeast from sugar-cane molasses or from molasses of sweet plant juices combined with rice — presented in containers of a capacity not exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN subheading 2208 90 11.

coverage_explanation:
The proposed axioms capture the four key discriminating criteria for CN 2208 90 11:
1. The product is an arrack spirit (ArrackSpiritQuality — encoding the special yeast, sugar-cane molasses / sweet plant-juice molasses + rice origin that distinguishes it from rum/sugar-cane spirits under 2208 40 and from raki under 2208 90 56/90).
2. The production process is the arrack distillation process (ArrackDistillationProcess — a specialisation of SpiritousDistillationProcess).
3. The container volume is ≤ 2 litres (SmallContainer), discriminating 22089011 from its sibling 22089019 (> 2 l).
4. The alcoholic strength is less than 80% vol, placing it under heading 2208 rather than 2207.

Coverage gap (~15%): The legal note also explicitly excludes raki (redistilled raisin/dry-fig spirits with aniseed), which falls under 22089056/22089090. This exclusion is described in natural language in the note but cannot be fully encoded as a DL complement restriction without a dedicated RakiSpiritQuality class (not yet in the TBox). A complement restriction against a RakiSpiritQuality could be added in a future iteration. The aniseed/raisin/dried-fig origin distinction that separates arrack from raki is noted but not yet axiomatised.
